themes.exampleQuote: Sahil has been very kind & helpful with accepting custom orders whenever I come with my friends.
The staff were extremely rude and dismissive, which made the atmosphere feel unwelcoming.
Tried Indian food for the first time and I loved it.
Reva Modern Indian Cuisine is hands down our number one restaurant in Pittsburgh.
The person taking the order has the audacity to ask if it was my first time in the US.

notable_quote: Reva Modern Indian Cuisine is hands down our number one restaurant in Pittsburgh — the food is absolutely delicious and the service is excellent every single time we visit.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers return repeatedly and use superlatives freely about the food and specific staff like Sahil, but a hard cluster of complaints about front-of-house condescension — one incident involving a comment about a customer's nationality — is punching above its weight in a 527-review pool.
quarter_narrative: Thirty creators filmed Reva this quarter and posted nothing back to them. The business has 161 million TikTok plays in its orbit — almost entirely from a single @travelicious88 video that, on closer inspection, was shot in Kolkata, not East Liberty, and tagged the concept loosely. Strip that outlier and the creator signal is still real: 29 individual creators posted from or about Reva, and the business has been silent on Instagram for 71 days with no TikTok presence of its own. Reva holds the top rank among Pittsburgh Indian restaurants and sits fourth in the Pittsburgh Asian Kitchens family, behind Mola, which built its position in part on creator collaboration fit — the same axis where Reva has raw material but no infrastructure to meet creators halfway. The rank stays here as long as the Instagram stays dark; the moment Reva starts posting with any consistency, the engagement rate it already has — nearly 38 percent on a dormant account — will make the climb fast.
tldr_read: Dominant review volume and a 38-percent Instagram engagement rate on a dormant account, undercut by 71 days of silence and a service sentiment thread that won't stay buried. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Indian restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The conversion path is airtight and the creator interest is already there; the rank ceiling is the Instagram silence and a service narrative that needs more counter-evidence in the review pool to stop compounding.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Thirty Creators Already Filming, The Business Dormant On Instagram For Seventy-One Days.

playbook.headline: Break the 71-day Instagram silence now
playbook.action: Post a minimum of two reels per week to activate the existing 37.85% engagement rate before the account loses algorithmic standing entirely.

playbook.headline: Get ahead of the service narrative
playbook.action: The two rude-staff reviews are few but vivid and specific; a visible owner response and a run of staff-feature content directly addresses the 69 sentiment reading before it pulls the rank further.

playbook.headline: Meet the 30 creators already filming
playbook.action: Identify the top Pittsburgh-based creators among the 30 who posted this quarter and invite them in for a set visit; Mola built its family-leader position on exactly this axis.
